title: Binary Matrix Restoration Backtracking Bug Fix

statement.markdown: You are given a binary matrix. Your task is to restore the original matrix such that the number of 0s in each row and column is minimized. You are allowed to swap two opposite corners of the matrix at most once. Write a function to check if the matrix can be restored as specified above and print the final matrix if it can be restored.
statement.inputFormat: The input contains a square matrix (n x n) of 0s and 1s.
statement.outputFormat: Print Yes if the matrix can be restored, print the final matrix otherwise. Print No if the number of 0s in each row and column is already minimal.
statement.notes: Input matrix is guaranteed to be a square matrix.

examples.explanation: The original matrix can be restored.

hints.text: Check if the matrix can be restored by checking if the number of 0s in each row and column is already minimal.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: The idea is to first count the number of 0s in each row and column. If the number of 0s in each row and column is already minimal, then it is not possible to restore the matrix.
editorial.betterSolution: We can solve this problem using backtracking. Start with an empty matrix and try to fill it with 0s and 1s. At each step, check if the number of 0s in each row and column is still minimal. If it is, then continue filling the matrix.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can use a backtracking approach to solve this problem. Start with an empty matrix and try to fill it with 0s and 1s. At each step, check if the number of 0s in each row and column is still minimal. If it is, then continue filling the matrix. If not, then backtrack and try a different option.
commonMistakes: Not checking if the number of 0s in each row and column is minimal before filling the matrix.
